feature: hex-agent One-Shot Execution Mode for Workplan Tasks
description: Three bugs prevent workplan tasks from doing real work: (1) spawn_agent passes --agent-name but hex-agent CLI uses --agent — clap rejects the flag and exits immediately; (2) CliAdapter reads stdin line-by-line so multi-line prompts become many separate messages; (3) spawn_agent never awaits the child process so tasks 'complete' before any work is done. This workplan fixes all three and adds wait_for_completion semantics to SpawnConfig.

tasks.name: Fix spawn_agent to pass --agent matching hex-agent CLI flag
tasks.description: In hex-nexus/src/orchestration/agent_manager.rs, the spawn_agent function builds the hex-agent command with `cmd.arg("--agent-name").arg(&agent_name)`. But hex-agent/src/main.rs defines the flag as `#[arg(long)] agent: Option<String>` which clap maps to `--agent`, not `--agent-name`. Clap rejects unknown flags and exits immediately, so no work is ever done. Fix: change `cmd.arg("--agent-name")` to `cmd.arg("--agent")` in hex-nexus/src/orchestration/agent_manager.rs. Also check all other flags passed: --project-dir, --model, --hub-url, --hub-token all appear correct. Done when: cargo check -p hex-nexus passes.

phases.name: Add --prompt flag and one-shot mode to hex-agent

tasks.name: Add --prompt flag to hex-agent Args and one-shot execution path in CliAdapter
tasks.description:
Two changes in hex-agent:

1. In hex-agent/src/main.rs, add to the Args struct: `#[arg(long)] prompt: Option<String>` — a task prompt for non-interactive one-shot execution. When --prompt is provided, pass it through to CliAdapter.

2. In hex-agent/src/adapters/primary/cli.rs, update CliAdapter::run() to accept an optional prompt parameter: `pub async fn run(&self, prompt: Option<String>) -> anyhow::Result<()>`. If prompt is Some: skip the REPL loop entirely — send the full prompt as a single message via self.conversation.send_message(), print the response to stdout, then return Ok(()). If prompt is None AND stdin is not a TTY (use `atty::is(atty::Stream::Stdin)` — add atty crate to hex-agent/Cargo.toml): read ALL of stdin with `io::read_to_string(stdin)` as a single string, send as one message, return. If prompt is None AND stdin IS a TTY: run the existing interactive REPL loop unchanged.

This ensures: workplan executor writes multi-line prompt to stdin, closes pipe (EOF), agent reads it all as one message, processes it, exits cleanly. Done when: cargo check -p hex-agent passes; `echo 'say hello' | hex-agent --no-preflight` sends a single message and exits.

tasks.name: Add wait_for_completion to SpawnConfig; spawn_agent awaits child exit when set
tasks.description:
In hex-nexus/src/orchestration/agent_manager.rs:

1. SpawnConfig already has `prompt` and `worktree_branch` fields (added in previous work). Add `wait_for_completion: bool` with serde default false.

2. In spawn_agent(), after writing the prompt to stdin, if config.wait_for_completion is true: call `let exit_status = child.wait().await.map_err(|e| format!("Agent wait failed: {}", e))?;`. If exit_status.success(), return Ok(instance). If not, update agent status to Failed via state port and return Err(format!("hex-agent exited with {}", exit_status)).

3. In workplan_executor.rs execute_phase(), set `wait_for_completion: true` in the SpawnConfig built for workplan tasks.

4. The two ad-hoc SpawnConfig sites in chat.rs and orchestration.rs already have `wait_for_completion` absent — add `wait_for_completion: false` to both (fire-and-forget daemon mode unchanged).

Done when: cargo check -p hex-nexus passes; workplan execution blocks per task until agent exits.

tasks.name: Build both crates, smoke-test one-shot mode end-to-end
tasks.description:
Build and verify the full chain:

1. Run: cargo build -p hex-agent -p hex-nexus — both must compile.

2. Smoke test one-shot mode: `echo 'Print the number 42 and nothing else' | ./target/debug/hex-agent --no-preflight 2>/dev/null` — verify it prints 42 and exits (exit code 0). If ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set, skip this step and note it.

3. Verify --agent flag works: `./target/debug/hex-agent --agent hex-coder --help` — must not show 'unrecognized argument' error.

4. Check that all compilation warnings are reasonable (no new errors introduced).

Done when: both crates build, --agent flag accepted, one-shot stdin mode exits cleanly.
